title: Probability
short: A number from 0 to 1 giving how likely an event is; for equally likely outcomes it is favourable ÷ total.
full:
Probability measures how likely an event is, on a scale from 0 (impossible) to 1 (certain). When every outcome is equally likely, P(event) = (number of favourable outcomes) / (total number of outcomes), which is why counting techniques matter.

Probabilities of all outcomes sum to 1, and the probability of an event not happening is 1 − P(event). Combinations and permutations supply the favourable and total counts in many problems.
